description.s: The context menu is Hobson's primary interaction surface. Right-clicking any item opens a menu built by
, with sections for view management, item operations, and debugging.
Here is the actual context menu rendered for a Note item, built from the same code the viewport uses:
— visually identical to the interactive version, but without click handlers. This is the real menu, not a screenshot or mockup.
Adds a new or existing item as an attachment of the right-clicked item.
— opens the type picker, creates a new item of the chosen type
— opens a search modal to find and attach an existing item
Lists all views registered for the item's type. The currently active view shows a checkmark. Selecting a different view switches the item's rendering.
so the wrapper knows what content view to use underneath.
, the View As entry becomes a submenu showing the view's own options (e.g., spatial-canvas-view's layout toggles).
Opens a modal showing the three-level view preference hierarchy. Here is the actual panel rendered for the Note type definition:
The view-resolution levels, in priority order, are: context (this location only) -> item (wherever it appears) -> type (all items of this type) -> system default.
— copies the item's GUID to clipboard
— navigates to the item as viewport root, preserving view config
Debug views (like Raw JSON) and system tools:
— toggles the DOM inspector overlay (requires Debug Mode)
acts as a thin orchestrator — it calls library functions and handles positioning.

for functional transclusion. Each builder takes an
is null (as in this documentation), the menu renders inert.
— the viewport that hosts the menu
